title: O(N²) correlation matrix layout thrash on portfolio recompute
body:
**Issue:** CorrelationMatrix.tsx renders an O(N²) table layout on every positions recompute. With 100+ position portfolios, the matrix renders 10,000+ cells and recalculates correlations and styling on every quote update, triggering main-thread layout recalculations.

**Evidence:**
- CorrelationMatrix.tsx:41-47 spawns N useQueries in a flat array, triggering N re-renders on quotes updates
- Line 117-133: matrix.map() → row.map() creates nested JSX that forces full re-layout on color/value changes
- correlationMatrix() computation is not memoized with useMemo despite calling avgOffDiagonal() on every render
- staleTime: 30 * 60_000 (30min) is too long; quotes should refetch every 60s for active portfolios

**Root cause:**
1. useQueries dependency on tickers causes re-renders when any quote stales
2. No React.memo on cell component; each cell re-renders and recalculates color
3. Layout thrashing: reading and writing layout in tight loop during table render

**Impact:** Portfolio view becomes jank when watching >50 positions. CPU spikes every minute.

**Fix:** 
- Wrap matrix cell in React.memo with shallow equality
- Memoize correlationMatrix and avgOffDiagonal results
- Increase refetchInterval to 5 * 60_000 for less frequent quote updates
- Consider virtualized grid (react-window) if >100 positions

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/CorrelationMatrix.tsx

title: Query client staleTime=60s too aggressive for slow-changing data
body:
**Issue:** AppProviders.tsx sets global defaultOptions.queries.staleTime = 60_000 (1 minute), but many endpoints serve slow-changing data (fundamentals, statements, ownership) that only change daily or weekly. This causes aggressive refetches and network churn.

**Evidence:**
- src/renderer/src/providers/AppProviders.tsx:10: staleTime: 60_000 global default
- useStatements.tsx:55: overrides to 30min (good), but other hooks inherit 60s default
- useFundamentals.tsx:78: manually set to 15min (good)
- useOwnership.tsx:103: manually set to 60min (good)
- But useNews.tsx:162, useFilings.tsx:182 use quote-based 60s staleness for filing data that changes monthly

**Root cause:**
1. Global default doesn't account for data freshness requirements
2. Inconsistent staleTime across hooks; no documented strategy
3. Desktop Electron app has no offline-first strategy, so aggressive refetch is unnecessary

**Impact:** Excess IPC calls to backend, battery drain on laptop, backend load spike every 60s.

**Fix:**
- Change global default staleTime to 5 * 60_000 (5min)
- Document staleTime strategy: quotes=60s, dailies=30min, weeklies=2h
- Use queryKey hash strategy to batch related queries
- Set refetchOnWindowFocus: false (already done, good)

File: src/renderer/src/providers/AppProviders.tsx

title: FinanceHome renders 12 eager panels; no lazy-load or splitting
body:
**Issue:** FinanceHome.tsx renders 12 dashboard panels (MarketIndexCards, SectorHeatmap, WatchlistHeatmap, SectorRotation, MacroCalendar, etc.) eagerly within ResponsiveGrid. Each panel has its own useQueries calls, and the entire set is loaded on module import without lazy boundaries.

**Evidence:**
- FinanceHome.tsx:102-222: 12 hardcoded panel divs, each with <ErrorBoundary> wrapping sub-component
- Lines 108, 118, 128, etc.: MarketIndexCards, MacroIndicators, TopMovers all imported at top, not lazy
- Each panel calls useQueries() independently; no request batching
- react-grid-layout overhead for managing layout state (onLayoutChange callback on every drag)

**Root cause:**
1. Panels imported as named imports, bundled together
2. Dashboard renders all panels even if scrolled out of view
3. No intersection observer to lazy-render panels below the fold
4. react-grid-layout recalculates layout on every resize; no debounce

**Impact:** FinanceHome initial render is slow (~2-3s). Memory footprint high due to all 12 panel DOM nodes. Users see white screen while loading.

**Fix:**
- Lazy-load panels below the fold using React.lazy + Suspense
- Defer MarketSummary, MacroCalendar, FearGreed panels (AI generation is slow)
- Add useMemo() wrapper around ResponsiveGrid layouts to prevent unnecessary re-layouts
- Batch MarketIndexCards + TopMovers into single useQueries call
- Debounce onLayoutChange handler with 500ms threshold

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/FinanceHome.tsx

title: StockDetail loads all 11 tabs eagerly without code splitting
body:
**Issue:** StockDetail.tsx imports all 11 detail tabs (OverviewTab, FinancialsTab, TechnicalsTab, OptionsTab, etc.) as named imports and renders them with conditional logic. Tabs that fetch heavy data (Financials, Options, Ownership) are loaded into memory even if the user never opens them.

**Evidence:**
- StockDetail.tsx:1-18: Lines 7-16 import all tabs (OverviewTab, AnalystPanel, FinancialsTab, TechnicalsTab, EarningsTab, OwnershipTab, PeersTab, OptionsTab, NewsTab, SentimentTab, SimulationTab)
- Lines 57-67: Conditional rendering; unvisited tabs still loaded and parsed
- OverviewTab.tsx alone loads 13 sub-components (InteractiveChart, FundamentalsGrid, NewsPanel, etc.)
- FinancialsTab likely calls useStatements + useQueries → network churn

**Root cause:**
1. All tabs imported statically; no lazy() wrapper
2. Tab switching doesn't lazy-load on first open
3. Analysts tab (line 58) fetches research data without prefetch or debounce

**Impact:** Stock detail page initial load is 800-1200ms slower than necessary. Switching to Financials tab has 500ms lag waiting for FinancialsTab chunk.

**Fix:**
- Wrap tab imports in React.lazy():
  - FinancialsTab = lazy(() => import('./tabs/FinancialsTab'))
  - OptionsTab, OwnershipTab, SentimentTab similarly
- Keep OverviewTab eager (most common)
- Add Suspense fallback with spinner
- Prefetch likely next tab on hover (shift next tab into cache)

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/StockDetail.tsx

title: useQuotes missing request deduplication across components
body:
**Issue:** useQuotes() (useFinance.ts:48-57) returns useQueries results without stable queryKey arrays. Multiple components calling useQuotes(same tickers array) spawn duplicate network requests instead of sharing cache.

**Evidence:**
- useFinance.ts:48-57: useQuotes maps tickers to queryKey ['quote', t] per ticker, but caller controls order
- PositionsList.tsx:13: calls useQuotes(tickers) → if order changes, cache misses
- MarketIndexCards.tsx:46: calls useQuotes(indices.map(i => i.symbol)) → queryKey order = UI order, not normalized
- WatchlistHeatmap.tsx:19: calls useQuotes(tickers) → tickers order from DB, unstable
- No deduplication layer; if MarketIndexCards + PositionsList both request '^GSPC', two requests fire

**Root cause:**
1. queryKey array order depends on input order, not canonical symbol order
2. No QueryClient.prefetchQuery to batch requests
3. No query dependencies; each ticker is independent

**Impact:** Network overhead; 2-3 duplicate requests per symbol loaded in multiple panels. Backend sees 50% more load than necessary.

**Fix:**
- Normalize queryKey in useQuotes: [...tickers].sort() before mapping
- Create useQuoteBatch() helper that deduplicates and batches by symbol
- Use queryClient.prefetchQuery(queryKey) to eagerly load common symbols (S&P indices)
- Implement query suspense and streaming for first load

File: src/renderer/src/hooks/useFinance.ts

title: InteractiveChart memory leak on chart type/range switching
body:
**Issue:** InteractiveChart.tsx creates lightweight-charts instance in useEffect and attempts cleanup, but switching chart type (Area ↔ Candle) or range doesn't fully release the old series. chartRef persists across renders; priceSeriesRef recreated but old series DOM nodes linger.

**Evidence:**
- InteractiveChart.tsx:38-71: createChart() called once, cleanup on unmount only
- Lines 73-100: useEffect for kind change; removeSeries + addSeries pattern, but no chart.remove()
- Line 76: removeSeries called but old chart instance still holds DOM references
- No useCallback for range/kind handlers; new function created every render
- Lines 102-135: setData called on every bars update; no debounce
- lightweight-charts version 5.1.0 is recent; should check memory profiling

**Root cause:**
1. Chart instance created once, kept for lifetime of component
2. Series removed but not fully garbage-collected
3. No memory profiling to detect DOM node leaks
4. setData() called synchronously on every bars array change

**Impact:** Switching between chart types 10+ times creates memory pressure; slow performance on M1 MacBook. Long-lived stock detail tabs accumulate ~5-10MB per tab over 30min.

**Fix:**
- Add cleanup for series removal: ensure volumeSeriesRef also removed on kind change
- Call chartRef.current.remove() on dependency change if necessary
- Debounce setData() calls with useMemo: only update when bars array length/hash changes
- Use useCallback for handlers to avoid re-render
- Profile with React DevTools Profiler tab; confirm memory release

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/InteractiveChart.tsx

title: PositionsList spawns N parallel quote requests on load
body:
**Issue:** PositionsList.tsx:13 calls useQuotes(tickers) which spawns N individual useQuery calls via useQueries. If user has 50 positions, 50 parallel network requests fire simultaneously, overwhelming backend and UI thread.

**Evidence:**
- PositionsList.tsx:13: const quotes = useQuotes(tickers) with no batching
- useFinance.ts:48-57: useQuotes returns useQueries.map, one per ticker
- Lines 15-23: enriched.map re-maps and calculates totals on every quote update
- No staleTime override; inherits 60s default → refetch surge every 60s
- No loading skeleton; table jumps as prices arrive async

**Root cause:**
1. useQuotes spawns parallel requests without limiting concurrency
2. enriched array recalculated on every partial quote arrival
3. No waterfall detection; requests fire unthrottled

**Impact:** Portfolio page startup is 3-5s slow due to request queue. UI jank as 50 quote updates stream in asynchronously. High CPU on poor networks.

**Fix:**
- Implement request batching: create useQuotesBatch() that combines tickers into single backend call
- Limit concurrency: use maxConcurrent: 5 in queryOptions
- Add loading skeleton while quotes arrive
- Increase staleTime to 30_000 for portfolio context (prices don't change every 60s)
- Memoize enriched calculation: useMemo with dependency on [positions, quotes] only

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/PositionsList.tsx

title: Dashboard panels spawn 30+ parallel requests without batching
body:
**Issue:** SectorRotation.tsx and WatchlistHeatmap.tsx both call useQueries to fetch historical data for sectors and watchlist items independently. If both panels are visible on dashboard, 12+ parallel historical requests fire, overwhelming backend.

**Evidence:**
- SectorRotation.tsx:52-58: useQueries with 12 sector SECTORS (XLK, XLF, XLE, ..., SPY)
- WatchlistHeatmap.tsx:19: calls useQuotes(tickers) → if watchlist has 20+ items, 20 parallel quote requests
- FinanceHome.tsx renders both panels without deduplication
- No request batching; each panel independently fetches
- staleTime: 30 * 60_000 means refetch every 30min; dashboard refresh = cascade of 32 requests

**Root cause:**
1. No shared query layer; each panel independently manages data fetching
2. No request deduplication key (e.g., if both panels request SPY quotes)
3. No priority/concurrency control

**Impact:** Dashboard load cascades 30+ requests in <1s. Backend sees request spike. First dashboard render is 5-8s vs ideal 1-2s.

**Fix:**
- Create useMarketData() hook that wraps common queries (quotes, historical) with deduplication
- Normalize all quote requests to single useQuotes call with combined ticker array
- Use queryClient.setQueryData to pre-populate related queries
- Prefetch sector data on lazy-load, not on render
- Set maxNetworkConcurrency: 5 in QueryClient config

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/SectorRotation.tsx, WatchlistHeatmap.tsx

title: TradesTable renders 500+ rows without virtualization
body:
**Issue:** TradesTable.tsx renders all trades in a <table> without virtualization. With 500+ trades, DOM node count exceeds 3000; scrolling frame rate drops to 20 fps.

**Evidence:**
- TradesTable.tsx:19-78: <table> with trades.map((t) => <tr>) rendering all rows
- No react-window or react-virtual wrapper
- Each row renders Trash2 icon + onClick handler → garbage collection churn
- No key optimization; using array index implicit key (bug waiting to happen)
- CSS has no will-change or contain properties

**Root cause:**
1. Naive DOM rendering; no virtualization
2. All 500 rows parsed, rendered, mounted in initial render
3. Scroll event listener causes layout recalculation per frame

**Impact:** Users with >200 trades experience stuttering scroll (20 fps vs 60 fps). 5-second initial render.

**Fix:**
- Replace <table> with react-window FixedSizeList or TanStack react-table with virtualization
- Render 50 rows at a time; rest in virtual scroll buffer
- Memoize TradeRow component with React.memo to prevent unnecessary re-renders
- Add key={t.id} instead of array index
- Add contain: layout CSS to isolate row repaints

File: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradesTable.tsx

title: Build config missing code-splitting and chunk size limits
body:
**Issue:** electron.vite.config.ts does not configure chunk splitting or chunk size limits for the renderer bundle. With 15+ modules (finance, sports, health, pdf, assistant) and lazy route imports, the main bundle could be bloated if code-splitting isn't enforced.

**Evidence:**
- electron.vite.config.ts:6-24: minimal config; no vite build.rollupOptions
- package.json:30-45: dependencies include heavy libs (lightweight-charts, pdf-lib, react-grid-layout)
- router.tsx uses lazy() for routes, but no build config ensures separate chunks
- No manualChunks strategy to split vendor, app, modules

**Root cause:**
1. Vite defaults to auto-splitting, but no explicit chunk size thresholds
2. No vendor chunk for node_modules; might bundle all in one JS
3. No module federation or dynamic import strategy

**Impact:** Renderer bundle could be 800KB+ uncompressed; first load slow on slow disk/CPU. No ability to hot-reload modules independently.

**Fix:**
- Add rollupOptions.output.manualChunks:
  - vendor chunk: node_modules dependencies
  - react chunk: React + react-router
  - finance module chunk: finance/* routes
  - ui chunk: shared components
- Set chunkSizeWarningLimit: 400
- Enable dynamic import for lazy routes
- Profile bundle with vite --profile; use rollup-plugin-visualizer to identify bloat

File: electron.vite.config.ts
